{{Shinto Shrine |name = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamitani shrine, Kumihama, Kyotango 2019-08-12(1) sa.jpg|280px]]
Kamitani Shrine grounds |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyotango]], [[Kumihama]] Town, Kotani |coordinates = {{Coord|35|36|14|N|134|53|31|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] (Kamitani Shrine)
[[Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] (Tachimiyajinja Shrine) |Sacred Object = Kunimi Sword |type = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Gōsha]] |Establishment = Ancient times |Main Hall = Two-bay irimoya-zukuri style with kara-hafu and cypress bark roof |alternate_name = Tachimiyajinja |Festival = [[October 17]] (now held on the second Sunday of October) |Rituals = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival }} '''Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' (神谷太刀宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the southwestern region of [[Kumihama]], [[Kyotango City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango]] [[Category:History of Kyotango]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]]

The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango]] [[Category:History of Kyotango]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]]

{{Shinto Shrine |name = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamitani shrine, Kumihama, Kyotango 2019-08-12(1) sa.jpg|280px]]
Kamitani Shrine grounds |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyotango]], [[Kumihama]] Town, Kotani |coordinates = {{Coord|35|36|14|N|134|53|31|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] (Kamitani Shrine)
[[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] (Tachimiyajinja Shrine) |Sacred Object = Kunimi Sword |type = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Gōsha]] |Establishment = Ancient times |Main Hall = Two-bay irimoya-zukuri style with kara-hafu and cypress bark roof |alternate_name = Tachimiyajinja |Festival = [[October 17]] (now held on the second Sunday of October) |Rituals = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival }} '''Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' (神谷太刀宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the southwestern region of [[Kumihama]], [[Kyotango City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango]] [[Category:History of Kyotango]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]]

Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango]] [[Category:History of Kyotango]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]

Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango]] [[Category:History of Kyotango]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]]

{{Shinto Shrine |name = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamitani shrine, Kumihama, Kyotango 2019-08-12(1) sa.jpg|280px]]
Kamitani Shrine grounds |location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyotango]], [[Kumihama]] Town, Kotani |coordinates = {{Coord|35|36|14|N|134|53|31|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] (Kamitani Shrine)
[[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] (Tachimiyajinja Shrine) |Sacred Object = Kunimi Sword |type = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Gōsha]] |Establishment = Ancient times |Main Hall = Two-bay irimoya-zukuri style with kara-hafu and cypress bark roof |alternate_name = Tachimiyajinja |Festival = [[October 17]] (now held on the second Sunday of October) |Rituals = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival }} '''Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' (神谷太刀宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the southwestern region of [[Kumihama]], [[Kyotango City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango]] [[Category:History of Kyotango]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyotango, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]

Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]

While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |name = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamitani shrine, Kumihama, Kyotango 2019-08-12(1) sa.jpg|280px]]
Kamitani Shrine grounds |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyotango|Kyotango]], [[Kumihama]] Town, Kotani |coordinates = {{Coord|35|36|14|N|134|53|31|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] (Kamitani Shrine)
[[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] (Tachimiyajinja Shrine) |Sacred Object = Kunimi Sword |type = [[Shikinaisha]], former [[Gōsha]] |Establishment = Ancient times |Main Hall = Two-bay irimoya-zukuri style with kara-hafu and cypress bark roof |alternate_name = Tachimiyajinja |Festival = [[October 17]] (now held on the second Sunday of October) |Rituals = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival }} '''Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' (神谷太刀宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the southwestern region of [[Kumihama]], [[Kyotango City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shinto Shrine |name = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamitani shrine, Kumihama, Kyotango 2019-08-12(1) sa.jpg|280px]]
Kamitani Shrine grounds |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyotango|Kyotango]], [[Kumihama]] Town, Kotani |coordinates = {{Coord|35|36|14|N|134|53|31|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] (Kamitani Shrine)
[[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] (Tachimiyajinja Shrine) |Sacred Object = Kunimi Sword |type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}}, former [[Gōsha]] |Establishment = Ancient times |Main Hall = Two-bay irimoya-zukuri style with kara-hafu and cypress bark roof |alternate_name = Tachimiyajinja |Festival = [[October 17]] (now held on the second Sunday of October) |Rituals = Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival }} '''Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' (神谷太刀宮神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the southwestern region of [[Kumihama]], [[Kyotango City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[:en:Yomiuri Shimbun|Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Originally separate shrines, '''Kamitani Shrine''' and '''Tachimiyajinja Shrine''' were later merged. Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[:en:Yomiuri Shimbun|Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[:en:Yomiuri Shimbun|Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]

Known as "Tachimiyajinja" during the Edo period, it became revered as a [[Gōsha]] of Kumihama during the Meiji period.{{Harvnb|Kyoto Shrines|1984|p=212}} As the guardian deity of Kumihama Ichiku, it is affectionately referred to as "Tachimiyajinja." Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[:en:Yomiuri Shimbun|Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]

Its autumn festival is one of the prominent ceremonies in the Tango region.{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|p=231}} Kamitani Shrine is considered one of the 11 shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] of Kumano District. Whether the original Shikinaisha included Tachimiyajinja remains unclear.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=69}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/1/3404.html |title=Kamitani Shrine Main Hall (Prefectural Cultural Asset) |publisher=Kyotango City |accessdate=2020-12-08}} Tachimiyajinja uniquely enshrines [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] as the primary deity,{{Harvnb|Kumihama Guide|2019|p=9}} and the "Kunimi Sword" sacred to the shrine is linked to the etymology of the name "Kumihama."{{Harvnb|Kumano District Gazette|1923|pp=298–299}} == Overview == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 磐座.jpg|thumb|left|Visitors imitating the pose of the protagonist of ''Demon Slayer'' (December 2020)]] The shrine is deeply tied to the ancient history of the Tango region and the legacy of [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]]. Its association with the Kunimi Sword has fostered many sword-related traditions and donations. Following the popularity of ''Demon Slayer'' in 2020, its sacred rocks drew attention for resembling those in the series.{{Cite news|title=Sacred Rocks Resembling ''Demon Slayer'' Gain Popularity |newspaper=[[:en:Yomiuri Shimbun|Yomiuri Shimbun]] |date=2020-12-6 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20201206-OYT1T50091/ |accessdate=2020-12-07}}{{Cite news|title=Kyotango Shrine's Rocks Compared to ''Demon Slayer'' |newspaper=[[Sankei News]] |date=2020-12-3 |author=Tanaka Yukimi |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201203-GL5W4YKD7VKDPBYZ3V7TX763ZA/}} Initially, the Kamitani and Tachimiyajinja shrines were separate entities. Kamitani Shrine was originally located in the forest of Kumihama Town's Kandani district. Due to its collapse, it was merged with Tachimiyajinja Shrine in the town's center. Although recorded in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Kamitani Shrine," it has long been referred to as "Tachimiyajinja" in local traditions and historical documents. Today, it remains a beloved landmark and the guardian shrine of Kumihama Ichiku, with its annual autumn festival recognized as a major event in Kyotango City. The shrine's main hall, constructed in the ''Ōyashiro-zukuri'' style, and the "Reference Hall," partially built using materials from the former [[Kumihama Prefecture]] government office, are designated Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Assets. == History == [[File:Kamitani-tachinomiya 参道.jpg|thumb|Path to the shrine, unchanged since the Edo period.]] The shrine's origins trace back to the reign of Emperor Sujin (10th emperor). According to tradition, [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] prayed for success while inspecting the [[San’in Road]] and enshrined [[Yachimata-no-Kami]] brought from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]]. After completing the inspection, Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto settled in Kumihama, marrying a local noblewoman and fathering daughters who married into the imperial family.{{Harvnb|Kumihama Town Records|1975|p=55}} Upon his death during Emperor Suinin's reign, he was enshrined, and his sword became the shrine's sacred object, giving the name "Tachimiyajinja." In the Edo period, the shrine gained prominence, and documents from 1596 and 1603 record land donations by local rulers. Reconstructed in 1781, its main hall was modeled in a unique combination of Izumo architectural styles and Kyoto craftsmanship. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Yachimata-no-Kami]] (alternative name for [[Ōkuninushi]]) * [[Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto]] * [[:ja:天神玉命|Ame-no-Kamitama-no-Mikoto]] * [[天種子命|Ame-no-Tane-no-Mikoto]] (ancestral deity of the [[Nakatomi clan]]) == Sacred Objects == The Kunimi Sword, sacred to the shrine, is associated with the etymology of Kumihama. While it no longer exists, legends link it to the origins of the nearby [[Nyoiji Temple]]. == Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (1781): Two-bay irimoya-zukuri with a cypress bark roof and unique Izumo-inspired architecture. * '''Reference Hall''': Former Kumihama Prefecture Government Office building. == Festivals == The shrine hosts the annual "Kamitani-Tachimiyajinja Festival" (autumn festival), known for its traditional taiko drumming and processions. == Access == * '''A11ress''': Kumihama Town, Kyotango City, Kyoto Prefecture * '''Transportation''': 6-minute walk from Kumihama Station (Kyoto Tango Railway). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/3330.html Kyotango City Digital Museum] * [https://www.kyotango.gr.jp/sightseeing/699/ Kyotango Navi] {{translated page|ja|神谷太刀宮神社|version=101759839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]] [[sn:Kamiya Tachi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%A5%9E%E8%B0%B7%E5%A4%AA%E5%88%80%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:amiya Tachi Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tango Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[sn:Shikinaisha]][[sn:Kamitani-Tachi-no-Miya]][[sn:Shikinaisha]] {{Commons category|Kamitani-tachinomiya}} [[ja:神谷太刀宮神社]]
